facts.claim: Honningsvåg is the northernmost city/town in mainland Norway.

Honningsvåg ⓘ is the northernmost city/town[1] in mainland[4] Norway. It is located in Nordkapp Municipality in Finnmark county. Honningsvåg was declared a city in 1996, despite its small population. The 1.04-square-kilometre (260-acre) town has a population of 2,245 (2023), which gives the town a population density of 2,159 inhabitants per square kilometre (5,590/sq mi).[2]
